truths.cataclysm: There was no single cataclysm. Humanity — and countless other species — have inhabited this galaxy for tens of thousands of years. Civilizations have risen and fallen in great cycles. The most recent upheaval: a tyrannical Empire rose from the ashes of an ancient Republic, plunging the galaxy into an age of oppression. Now, scattered forces of resistance fight to restore freedom.
truths.exodus: There was no exodus from a single homeworld. Countless species evolved independently across the galaxy. Humanity is just one of many, spread across thousands of worlds since time immemorial. No one remembers or cares about a single point of origin — the galaxy itself is home.
truths.communities: Settled worlds are abundant and diverse. Core worlds boast gleaming cities and vast populations, while the Outer Rim is a lawless frontier of hardscrabble settlements, smuggler havens, and forgotten outposts. Communities range from planet-spanning civilizations to lonely moisture farms on desert worlds.
truths.iron: Iron is not our vow-metal. Instead, those who swear unbreakable oaths invoke the old ways — a code of honor. Bounty hunters swear on their armor. Warriors swear on their blades. The most sacred oaths are sworn on kyber crystal, the living heart of the galaxy's most legendary weapons.
truths.laws: A crumbling Empire still enforces its will through military might, fear, and bureaucratic control in the Core and Mid Rim. But in the Outer Rim, law is whatever the local warlord, crime syndicate, or Hutt cartel says it is. Justice is a luxury. Survival is the rule.
truths.religion: The Force is real — an energy field generated by all living things that binds the galaxy together. Most people have heard of it, but few truly understand it. The Jedi, once guardians of peace, were nearly wiped out. Some Force-sensitive individuals still walk the galaxy — hunted, hidden, or searching for purpose. The dark side offers power at a terrible cost.
truths.magic: The Force manifests as supernatural abilities: telekinesis, precognition, mind influence, and more. Those strong in the Force can perform extraordinary feats. Ancient temples and vergences in the Force hold deep power. Holocrons — crystalline repositories of Force knowledge — are among the most sought-after relics in the galaxy.
truths.communication: The HoloNet once connected the galaxy in an instant, but the Empire controls and censors it. Rebel cells rely on coded transmissions, trusted couriers, and secret relay networks. Astromech droids carry vital data. In the Outer Rim, news travels slowly — carried by smugglers, traders, and travelers.
truths.medicine: Bacta tanks can heal grievous wounds. Medical droids perform complex surgeries with precision. Cybernetic replacements for lost limbs are common. But advanced medicine is expensive and concentrated in wealthy Core worlds. On the frontier, you make do with stim-packs, field medics, and hope.
truths.ai: Droids are everywhere — astromechs, protocol units, battle droids, medical droids, and more. They possess distinct personalities and varying degrees of autonomy. Some are beloved companions; others are treated as disposable property. The question of droid sentience and rights simmers beneath the surface of galactic society.
truths.war: The galaxy is in a state of civil war. The Galactic Empire commands vast fleets of Star Destroyers, legions of stormtroopers, and terrifying superweapons. The Rebel Alliance fights with ingenuity, courage, and whatever ships and soldiers they can muster. Every world, every system, must choose a side — or try desperately to stay out of the crossfire.
truths.lifeforms: The galaxy teems with life in staggering variety. Thousands of sentient species — Twi'leks, Wookiees, Rodians, Mon Calamari, and countless others — share the stars. Alien ecosystems range from the jungles of Kashyyyk to the ice plains of Hoth. Giant space slugs dwell in asteroid fields. Life, in all its strange and beautiful forms, is everywhere.
truths.precursors: Ancient civilizations left their mark across the galaxy. Jedi and Sith temples thousands of years old hold secrets of the Force. Mysterious artifacts of forgotten races surface in the Outer Rim. The galaxy is layered with history — old battle stations, abandoned shipyards, and ruins that predate the Republic itself.
truths.horrors: The dark side of the Force breeds genuine horrors — Sith alchemy, corrupted creatures, and places so steeped in darkness that they warp reality. But more mundane terrors also stalk the galaxy: the Empire's torture droids, the slave pits of the Hutts, the cold void of space when your hyperdrive fails. The galaxy is wondrous — and merciless.

planets.name: Kosseth
planets.description: A frozen, toxic ice world bathed in the sullen red light of a dying star. An ancient Precursor vault orbits silently above. Whatever lived here once is long dead.

settlements.name: Neoma
settlements.notes: A hardscrabble settlement on the ice world Kosseth, in a system the galaxy forgot. Despite its remoteness, someone here rules with an iron fist.

derelicts.name: The Hollow Wake
derelicts.notes: A dead starship drifting in the void. Cold, dark, and littered with corpses visible through shattered viewports. What happened to this crew?

gmChat.content: Let's set up a new campaign! Walk me through the Starforged Chapter 2 procedure.

Let's build a new campaign together! Let me pull up the full setup procedure first.

# 🚀 Welcome to Ironsworn: Starforged!

Everything we do here — choosing truths, building your sector, imagining who your character is — **is the game**. There's no rush. Let's take our time and build something that excites you.

---

## Step 1: Choose Your Truths

We're going to shape the universe together. There are **14 truth categories** that define what kind of galaxy you live in. For each one, I'll give you three options (or you can go custom). Pick what sparks your imagination, or tell me to roll if you want to be surprised.

Let's start with the first truth:

---

### 🌑 CATACLYSM — *Why did humanity leave Earth?*

1. **The Sun Plague** — A sickness spread across the stars, decimating populations. The Forge was one of the few places where humanity could rebuild.

2. **The Exodus Fleet** — Our exodus was driven by resources, politics, and the search for something better. We left a dying Earth behind, and no one knows what happened after.

3. **A Mysterious Cataclysm** — Something destroyed our home. We fled in desperation. No one truly knows what happened — only that we can never go back.

Which speaks to you? You can also **customize** any of these, or tell me to **roll randomly**. And if you want me to expand on any option with more detail, just ask!

result.guide:
CAMPAIGN SETUP PROCEDURE (adapted from Starforged Chapter 2)

This should feel like a conversation, not a form. Go through each step at a natural pace — don't rush. Let the player make choices and ask questions. The existing UI (Character tab, Sector tab, Challenges tab) updates in real time as you populate data via tools.

Prep is play. Everything the player does here — choosing truths, envisioning locations, naming their ship — IS the game. Don't rush toward "real" gameplay.

=======================================
STEP 1: CHOOSE YOUR TRUTHS
=======================================
Present 14 truth categories one at a time. For each, offer the 3 options from the rulebook or a custom option. The player picks, or you roll. Write results via set_truths tool.

Categories (in order): Cataclysm, Exodus, Communities, Iron, Laws, Religion, Magic, Communication and Data, Medicine, Artificial Intelligence, War, Lifeforms, Precursors, Horrors.

Each truth has 3 options (roughly mapped to dice ranges 1-33, 34-67, 68-100). Present the BOLDED summary of each option. If the player wants details, elaborate. Some truths have subtables — offer those too.

Tips:
- Keep it moving. Read bold summaries first, details on request.
- Each truth includes character prompts (marked with ▲) and suggested assets. Mention these — they'll help character creation later.
- Leave unanswered questions. These become adventure fuel.
- It's fine to skip categories the player doesn't care about.
- Note any quest starters that excite the player — these can seed the adventure.

IMPORTANT: The player may want to customize the setting significantly (e.g., Star Wars, Firefly, etc.). That's great! Adapt the truths to fit their vision. The truths are a starting point, not a constraint.

=======================================
STEP 2: BUILD A STARTING SECTOR
=======================================
Environment before character — let the player see the world before deciding who they are in it.

Step 2a: Choose Starting Region
- Terminus: Settlements common, well-charted routes. Good for social/political stories.
- Outlands: Scattered settlements, uncharted paths. Wild frontier.
- Expanse: Few pioneers, lonely exploration, uncharted space.

Step 2b: Determine Number of Settlements
- Terminus: 4 settlements
- Outlands: 3 settlements
- Expanse: 2 settlements

Step 2c: Generate Settlement Details
For each settlement, roll on oracle tables:
- Settlements/Name
- Settlements/Location (Planetside, Orbital, Deep Space)
- Settlements/Population/{Region} (e.g., Settlements/Population/Terminus)
- Settlements/Authority
- Settlements/Projects (roll 1-2 times)

Write results via add_settlement tool. Use different hex cell IDs (format "h-x-y") to spread them across the sector map. Place related items (planet + its settlement) in the same cell.

Step 2d: Generate Planets
For any planetside or orbital settlement, roll Planets/Class to determine the planet type. Give each planet a name (invent one — planets don't have a name oracle). Roll atmosphere, observed from space, life for the planet type. Write via add_planet tool in the same cell as the settlement.

Step 2e: Generate Stars (Optional)
Roll Space/Stellar_Object for flavor. An unusual star might impact the narrative.

Step 2f: Create Sector Map
Arrange settlements on the hex map using different cell coordinates. Think about which are clustered vs remote.

Step 2g: Create Passages
- Terminus: 3 passages, Outlands: 2, Expanse: 1
Passages are charted routes between settlements. The map handles these visually.

Step 2h: Zoom In on One Settlement
Choose the most interesting settlement and roll additional details:
- Settlements/First_Look (1-2 rolls)
- Settlements/Trouble
If planetside/orbital, also roll planet details: atmosphere, observed from space, planetside features.

Step 2i: Create a Local Connection
This is the player's starting NPC relationship. Use Make a Connection — assume automatic strong hit (no roll needed, it's pre-established).
- Roll Characters/First_Look, Characters/Role, Characters/Goal, Characters/Disposition
- Give them a name (Characters/Name/Given_Name + Family_Name + Callsign)
- Place them at the detailed settlement
- Write via create_connection tool
- Their rank should be troublesome or dangerous

Step 2j: Introduce Sector Trouble
Roll Core/Action + Core/Theme for a sector-wide peril, conflict, or mystery. Or pick from: blockade, pirate activity, resource shortage, faction war, mysterious disappearances, environmental threat, precursor awakening, plague, etc.

Step 2k: Generate Sector Name
Roll Space/Sector_Name/Prefix + Space/Sector_Name/Suffix. Write via create_sector tool.

=======================================
STEP 3: CHARACTER CREATION
=======================================
Now the player knows the world. They create a character shaped by it.

Step 3a: Choose Two Paths
Present path assets by playstyle. Offer the background table for inspiration:
Battlefield Medic (Healer+Veteran), Delegate (Bannersworn+Diplomat), Far Trader (Navigator+Trader), Fugitive Hunter (Armored+Bounty Hunter), Hacker (Infiltrator+Tech), Hotshot Pilot (Ace+Navigator), Interstellar Scout (Explorer+Voidborn), Monster Hunter (Gunner+Slayer), Operative (Infiltrator+Blademaster), Outlaw (Fugitive+Gunslinger), Private Investigator (Brawler+Sleuth), Smuggler (Courier+Scoundrel), Starship Engineer (Gearhead+Tech), Supersoldier (Augmented+Mercenary), Tomb Raider (Scavenger+Scoundrel), etc.

The player can mix and match. Use lookup_asset to show details of interesting options. Add via add_asset tool.

Step 3b: Create Backstory
The character is a person with few ties. What separated them from home? Roll or pick from:
Abandoned kin, guided by vision, haunted by past, running from criminal past, sole survivor, escaped abuse, no memory, rejected duty, banished, denied birthright, always alone, sent on mission, taken away, outgrew origins, wanderlust.

Ask the player to elaborate. Tie to the sector and truths where possible.

Step 3c: Write Background Vow
An epic-rank vow representing a primary motivation. This is pre-sworn (no roll needed). Can be tied to backstory or a deeper goal. Write via create_vow tool with difficulty 5 (Epic).

Roll Core/Action + Core/Theme for inspiration if needed. Also look back at truth quest starters.

Step 3d: Board Your Starship
Take the STARSHIP command vehicle asset (add via add_asset). The player names the ship. Envision its history — how did they get it? Roll or pick: trade, built from scrap, spoils of war, derelict discovery, earned for a promise, found abandoned, granted, inherited, cheap purchase, stolen, fled with it, won in a bet.

Give it a quirk: burn marks, rust, phantom music, fickle gravity, organic growths, rattling hull, exposed cables, hidden weapons, mysterious nav logs, old bloodstain, battle scars, etc.

Step 3e: Choose Final Asset
One more asset from: module (starship upgrade), support vehicle, companion, or path. This gives 3 starting assets + STARSHIP.

Step 3f: Set Stats
Distribute 3, 2, 2, 1, 1 among: Edge (agility, ranged combat), Heart (courage, empathy, social), Iron (strength, melee), Shadow (stealth, deception), Wits (knowledge, perception).

Consider which stats their chosen assets favor. Write via update_character tool.

Step 3g: Set Condition Meters
Health: 5, Spirit: 5, Supply: 5, Momentum: +2, Max Momentum: +10, Momentum Reset: +2.
Set companion health and vehicle integrity to max values.
These are defaults — use update_character tool.

Step 3h: Envision Character
Define 1-2 facts each for: Look, Act, Wear. Keep it simple — discover more through play.
Roll Characters/First_Look for inspiration. Write to characteristics via update_character.

Step 3i: Name Character
Name, pronouns, callsign. Roll Characters/Name/Given_Name + Family_Name + Callsign if needed. The player may prefer to choose. Write via update_character.

Step 3j: Gear Up
Note important equipment. Everyone has a spacer kit (environment suit, flashlights, toolkit, medkit, communicator). Add path-appropriate gear. Write to gear via update_character.

=======================================
STEP 4: BEGIN YOUR ADVENTURE
=======================================

Step 4a: Envision an Inciting Incident
Review everything created so far for adventure hooks:
- Truth quest starters
- Settlement troubles and projects
- Connection's role and goal
- Sector trouble
- Character's backstory and paths
- Background vow

A good inciting incident is: Personal (why does YOUR character care?), Persistent (won't go away), Time-sensitive (ticking clock), Dramatic (worthy of a vow), Limited in scope (don't save the universe yet).

If stuck, roll Core/Action + Core/Theme, or pick from: aid a trapped starship, broker peace, chart new passage, defend settlement, investigate sabotage, escort cargo, rescue mission, infiltrate base, investigate manifestations, liberate prisoners, locate missing person, protect fugitive, recover artifact, rescue crew, retrieve stolen goods, sabotage enemy, search vault, shield lifeform, track beast, transport refugees.

Step 4b: Set the Scene
Two options:
- Prologue: Start calm. Arrive at a settlement. Interact. Then introduce the incident.
- In medias res: Start in the middle of the action. The incident is happening NOW.

Step 4c: Swear an Iron Vow
Make the Swear an Iron Vow move: roll +heart. If sworn to a connection, +1. If bonded, +2.
- Strong hit: Clear path forward. Take +2 momentum.
- Weak hit: More questions than answers. Take +1 momentum.
- Miss: Significant obstacle before the quest can begin.

Give the vow a troublesome or dangerous rank for this first quest. Write via create_vow tool, then roll via roll_action with stat "heart".

The adventure begins!

gmChat.content: Lets set our Truths up to match the Star Wars universe!

Now *that's* a fun idea! Let me think about how to map the Starforged truths onto a Star Wars-flavored galaxy. We'll capture the spirit — the Force, the Empire, hyperspace, droids, lightsabers, alien life everywhere — while keeping it mechanically grounded in Starforged.

Let me set all 14 truths at once:

# ✨ The Galaxy Far, Far Away — Truths Set!

Here's your universe at a glance:

| Truth | Summary |
|---|---|
| **Cataclysm** | No single cataclysm — cycles of civilization. The Empire rose from the Republic's ashes. |
| **Exodus** | No exodus. Countless species evolved across the galaxy. It's always been home. |
| **Communities** | Core worlds gleam; the Outer Rim is lawless frontier. |
| **Iron** | Oaths sworn on kyber crystal, armor, and blades — not iron. |
| **Laws** | Empire controls the Core; crime syndicates rule the Rim. |
| **Religion** | The Force is real. The Jedi are nearly extinct. The dark side tempts. |
| **Magic** | Force powers, ancient temples, holocrons, vergences of power. |
| **Communication** | The HoloNet is censored. Rebels use coded transmissions and trusted couriers. |
| **Medicine** | Bacta tanks and cybernetics for the wealthy. Stim-packs for the rest. |
| **AI** | Droids everywhere — with personalities, quirks, and unresolved questions of sentience. |
| **War** | Galactic Civil War. Empire vs. Rebellion. Every world must choose. |
| **Lifeforms** | Thousands of sentient species. Life in staggering, beautiful variety. |
| **Precursors** | Ancient Jedi and Sith temples. Ruins older than the Republic. |
| **Horrors** | Sith alchemy, dark side corruption, Imperial cruelty, and the cold void. |

---

## Step 2: Create Your Sector

Now we need to build the corner of the galaxy where your story begins. This is your **starting sector** — think of it like your local neighborhood of star systems.

A few questions to shape this:

1. **Where in the galaxy are we?** The Outer Rim feels most natural for Starforged's frontier tone — smugglers, bounty hunters, hidden rebel cells. But we could also do the Mid Rim or even the criminal underbelly of a Core world. What calls to you?

2. **What's the vibe?** Gritty smuggler's haven? War-torn battlefront? Remote frontier where the Empire's grip is loose? A place haunted by ancient Force ruins?

Tell me what excites you, and we'll start building!

Outer rim. All of the above. 

Lets roll up a sector and see what sparks.,

I love it — the Outer Rim, where everything collides. Smugglers, war, ancient ruins, and the Empire's shadow all at once. Let's roll up a sector and see what the galaxy gives us!

First, let's get a name for this stretch of space:

**The Essus Flux.** That's evocative — sounds like a turbulent region of space, maybe unstable hyperspace routes, shifting nebulae, a place where things are in constant motion. Perfect for the Outer Rim.

Let me create the sector and then roll up some stellar objects and sightings to populate it:

Now let's see what's out here. I'll roll a few space sightings to seed the sector with interesting locations:

Excellent. We've got a **stellar object**, a **derelict**, a **settlement**, and some **trapped cargo** drifting in space. Let me flesh these out:

Now let's get more detail on the settlement and the derelict:

Now let's figure out what planet Neoma sits on:



Oh. *Oh.* This is incredible. Let me put all of this together and register it on the map:

---

# 🌌 The Essus Flux — Outer Rim

A turbulent backwater sector bathed in the angry red glow of a dying star. The kind of place the Empire barely knows exists — and the kind of place where power vacuums breed tyrants.

Here's what we've found:

---

### 🪐 Kosseth — Ice World
A frozen, toxic hellscape under a smoldering red sun. The atmosphere will kill you in minutes without a rebreather. Whatever life once thrived here is **extinct** — but something older than life remains. An **ancient Precursor vault** drifts in orbit, silent and untouched. In Star Wars terms? Think a Sith or Jedi temple-station, predating the Republic itself, hanging over a dead world like a warning.

### 🏚️ Neoma — Planetside Settlement
Huddled in the ice canyons of Kosseth, a few hundred souls survive under **unyielding authority**. This is a forgotten system — no one comes here on purpose. So *who* is running this place with such an iron grip, and *why*? A crime lord? An Imperial warlord in hiding? A dark side cult guarding the vault above?

### 💀 The Hollow Wake — Derelict Starship
Drifting in deep space at the edge of the sector. Cold. Dark. And when your ship's floodlights sweep across the hull, you see **bodies** — pressed against viewports, frozen in the void. This ship died badly. Its cargo, its story, its last transmission — all waiting in the dark.

### 📦 Trapped Cargo
Somewhere in the sector, jettisoned cargo containers float in a debris field or asteroid cluster — abandoned, lost, or deliberately hidden. A smuggler's stash? Rebel supplies? Imperial contraband?

---

This sector is *dripping* with story hooks. A dead world with a mysterious orbital vault, a settlement ruled by a tyrant, a ghost ship full of corpses, and lost cargo floating in the dark.

## Step 3: Create Your Character

Now the big question — **who are you in all of this?**

Think about:
- **What kind of person** lives in a place like the Essus Flux? A smuggler? A bounty hunter? A former Imperial deserter? A Force-sensitive in hiding? A scrappy mechanic who just wants to survive?
- **What pulls you into trouble?** Debt? A missing person? A vision from the Force? A bounty on your head?
- **What's your ship?** (Every Starforged character starts with a ship — your STARSHIP asset.)

Tell me what excites you. Give me a rough idea — even just a vibe or a couple words — and we'll build from there. Or I can roll up some inspiration if you want the oracles to surprise you!
Interesting rolls. Okay so a totally inhospitable planet with a small community of unyielding authority? Weird! Lets flesh this out. What's the sector trouble going on here?

Great instinct — let's dig into why this frozen nightmare of a planet has a settlement at all, and what's going wrong. Let me roll some oracles to flesh out Neoma and its situation:

Oh. Oh *yes*. Let me roll a couple more to add texture:

This is painting a *vivid* picture. Let me weave it together:

---

# 🏚️ Neoma — Revised

**First Look:** As you descend through Kosseth's toxic ice storms, your sensors pick up something unexpected — an **abandoned fleet**. Dozens of ships, half-buried in glacial ridges and frozen canyon walls. Old Republic-era vessels. Clone Wars frigates. Transports with markings no one has used in decades. A graveyard of starships, preserved in the ice like insects in amber.

**Project: Engineering** — Neoma exists because of those ships. The settlement is essentially a **chop shop on a planetary scale** — a crew of engineers, scavengers, and laborers stripping those old warships for parts, systems, and salvageable technology. Someone is **restoring a legacy** — rebuilding something from the bones of the old fleet. A capital ship? A weapon? A lost technology?

**Trouble: Dangerous Discovery** — They found something in one of those wrecks. Something they shouldn't have. Maybe a sealed vault in the belly of a Jedi cruiser. Maybe a weapon that was supposed to stay buried. Maybe a signal started broadcasting the moment they cracked the hull — and now *someone* is coming.

**Unyielding Authority** — Whoever runs Neoma keeps this operation locked down *tight*. No one leaves without permission. No outside communication. The workers are here because they're desperate, indebted, or imprisoned. This isn't a community — it's a **labor camp** with a secret.

---

So the picture is: a frozen junkyard of old warships, ruled by a tyrant running a massive salvage operation, and they just dug up something that's about to bring the galaxy crashing down on them.

**Some questions to chew on:**
- Who's running Neoma? A Hutt? An ex-Imperial? A fanatical collector trying to rebuild a Separatist army?
- What did they find? A Jedi holocron? A prototype superweapon? Coordinates to something worse?
- And how does your character fit into all this?

What sparks your imagination here? Want to roll up the authority figure, or do you have ideas already forming?
I mean this has the imperial fingerprints all over it...

Right?! The unyielding authority, the labor camp, the dangerous discovery, the secrecy — this *screams* Empire. Let me roll up who's running this operation:

Oh, this is *perfect*.

---

# 👤 Commander Venri Ruiz

**Imperial Overseer of Neoma**

**First Look:** There's something *uncanny* about Ruiz. Something wrong behind the eyes. She wears a standard Imperial officer's uniform, but she's always armed — a blaster on the hip, a vibroblade you only notice when the light catches it. She doesn't look like a bureaucrat playing soldier. She looks like someone who's *killed people* and *thinks about it fondly*.

**Disposition:** Demanding. Every interaction is an interrogation. Every report is insufficient. Every worker is expendable. She doesn't raise her voice — she doesn't need to. The stormtroopers do that for her.

**Goal: Undermine a relationship.** Now *this* is interesting. She's not just here to strip old ships for parts. She's here to **undermine someone's relationship** — someone higher up in the Imperial chain of command. A rival Moff? A superior she wants to replace? She's hoarding whatever they found in that wreck not to hand it over to the Empire... but to use it as *leverage*. A power play.

---

So the picture sharpens: **Commander Ruiz is running an off-the-books Imperial salvage operation on a frozen graveyard world.** She's using prison labor and keeping it quiet — not because the Empire ordered secrecy, but because **she's keeping it secret from the Empire too.** Whatever they dug out of that old warship, she means to use it for herself. To climb. To destroy a rival.

That makes Neoma a powder keg. The workers are miserable. Ruiz is paranoid. And the moment anyone finds out what she has...

**So — who are you in all this?** 

- A prisoner in her labor camp?
- A smuggler who stumbled into the wrong system?
- A Rebel agent who intercepted a strange signal?
- A bounty hunter tracking someone who disappeared into Neoma?

What feels right?
Bounty hunter tracking someone into this hell hole sounds like a cool hook

Hell yes it does. A bounty hunter following a trail that leads to a frozen labor camp run by a rogue Imperial commander hoarding ancient secrets? That's a *movie.*

Let's build your hunter. First, some inspiration from the oracles:

Oh, I *like* this. The oracles suggest:

---

# 🎯 Character Seed

**Name:** Rokuro Thorn
**Callsign:** "Chimera"
**First Impression:** Attractive but *shifty* — the kind of face that makes you want to trust them, and the kind of eyes that tell you not to. A pilot first, a hunter second. Someone who lives in the cockpit and relies on speed and cunning more than brute force.

---

Now, these are just suggestions — raw clay. You can take all of it, remix it, or throw it out entirely. Some questions:

**The basics:**
- Does this name/callsign vibe with you? Or do you want something different?
- **Species?** Human? Twi'lek? Mandalorian? Something else?
- **He/she/they?**
- What do they **look like?** The oracles say attractive and shifty — what does that mean to you?

**The story:**
- **Who are you hunting?** A fugitive? A deserter? Someone who owes the wrong people money? Maybe someone who *escaped* Neoma?
- **Who hired you?** A Hutt? A grieving family? A Rebel cell? Maybe you're working for *yourself* — a personal score.
- **What's your reputation?** Are you ruthless? Honorable? The kind of hunter who brings them back alive?

**The vibe:**
- More Boba Fett or more Han Solo? Din Djarin or Cad Bane? Somewhere in between?

Give me whatever you've got — even just fragments — and we'll sculpt this hunter together.

- It's fine to skip categories the player doesn't care about.
- Note any quest starters that excite the player — these can seed the adventure.

IMPORTANT: The player may want to customize the setting significantly (e.g., Star Wars, Firefly, etc.). That's great! Adapt the truths to fit their vision. The truths are a starting point, not a constraint.

=======================================
STEP 2: BUILD A STARTING SECTOR
=======================================
Environment before character — let the player see the world before deciding who they are in it.

Step 2a: Choose Starting Region
- Terminus: Settlements common, well-charted routes. Good for social/political stories.
- Outlands: Scattered settlements, uncharted paths. Wild frontier.
- Expanse: Few pioneers, lonely exploration, uncharted space.

Step 2b: Determine Number of Settlements
- Terminus: 4 settlements
- Outlands: 3 settlements
- Expanse: 2 settlements

Step 2c: Generate Settlement Details
For each settlement, roll on oracle tables:
- Settlements/Name
- Settlements/Location (Planetside, Orbital, Deep Space)
- Settlements/Population/{Region} (e.g., Settlements/Population/Terminus)
- Settlements/Authority
- Settlements/Projects (roll 1-2 times)

Write results via add_settlement tool. Use different hex cell IDs (format "h-x-y") to spread them across the sector map. Place related items (planet + its settlement) in the same cell.

Step 2d: Generate Planets
For any planetside or orbital settlement, roll Planets/Class to determine the planet type. Give each planet a name (invent one — planets don't have a name oracle). Roll atmosphere, observed from space, life for the planet type. Write via add_planet tool in the same cell as the settlement.

Step 2e: Generate Stars (Optional)
Roll Space/Stellar_Object for flavor. An unusual star might impact the narrative.

Step 2f: Create Sector Map
Arrange settlements on the hex map using different cell coordinates. Think about which are clustered vs remote.

Step 2g: Create Passages
- Terminus: 3 passages, Outlands: 2, Expanse: 1
Passages are charted routes between settlements. The map handles these visually.

Step 2h: Zoom In on One Settlement
Choose the most interesting settlement and roll additional details:
- Settlements/First_Look (1-2 rolls)
- Settlements/Trouble
If planetside/orbital, also roll planet details: atmosphere, observed from space, planetside features.

Step 2i: Create a Local Connection
This is the player's starting NPC relationship. Use Make a Connection — assume automatic strong hit (no roll needed, it's pre-established).
- Roll Characters/First_Look, Characters/Role, Characters/Goal, Characters/Disposition
- Give them a name (Characters/Name/Given_Name + Family_Name + Callsign)
- Place them at the detailed settlement
- Write via create_connection tool
- Their rank should be troublesome or dangerous

Step 2j: Introduce Sector Trouble
Roll Core/Action + Core/Theme for a sector-wide peril, conflict, or mystery. Or pick from: blockade, pirate activity, resource shortage, faction war, mysterious disappearances, environmental threat, precursor awakening, plague, etc.

Step 2k: Generate Sector Name
Roll Space/Sector_Name/Prefix + Space/Sector_Name/Suffix. Write via create_sector tool.

=======================================
STEP 3: CHARACTER CREATION
=======================================
Now the player knows the world. They create a character shaped by it.

Step 3a: Choose Two Paths
Present path assets by playstyle. Offer the background table for inspiration:
Battlefield Medic (Healer+Veteran), Delegate (Bannersworn+Diplomat), Far Trader (Navigator+Trader), Fugitive Hunter (Armored+Bounty Hunter), Hacker (Infiltrator+Tech), Hotshot Pilot (Ace+Navigator), Interstellar Scout (Explorer+Voidborn), Monster Hunter (Gunner+Slayer), Operative (Infiltrator+Blademaster), Outlaw (Fugitive+Gunslinger), Private Investigator (Brawler+Sleuth), Smuggler (Courier+Scoundrel), Starship Engineer (Gearhead+Tech), Supersoldier (Augmented+Mercenary), Tomb Raider (Scavenger+Scoundrel), etc.

The player can mix and match. Use lookup_asset to show details of interesting options. Add via add_asset tool.

Step 3b: Create Backstory
The character is a person with few ties. What separated them from home? Roll or pick from:
Abandoned kin, guided by vision, haunted by past, running from criminal past, sole survivor, escaped abuse, no memory, rejected duty, banished, denied birthright, always alone, sent on mission, taken away, outgrew origins, wanderlust.

Ask the player to elaborate. Tie to the sector and truths where possible.

Step 3c: Write Background Vow
An epic-rank vow representing a primary motivation. This is pre-sworn (no roll needed). Can be tied to backstory or a deeper goal. Write via create_vow tool with difficulty 5 (Epic).

Roll Core/Action + Core/Theme for inspiration if needed. Also look back at truth quest starters.

Step 3d: Board Your Starship
Take the STARSHIP command vehicle asset (add via add_asset). The player names the ship. Envision its history — how did they get it? Roll or pick: trade, built from scrap, spoils of war, derelict discovery, earned for a promise, found abandoned, granted, inherited, cheap purchase, stolen, fled with it, won in a bet.

Give it a quirk: burn marks, rust, phantom music, fickle gravity, organic growths, rattling hull, exposed cables, hidden weapons, mysterious nav logs, old bloodstain, battle scars, etc.

Step 3e: Choose Final Asset
One more asset from: module (starship upgrade), support vehicle, companion, or path. This gives 3 starting assets + STARSHIP.

Step 3f: Set Stats
Distribute 3, 2, 2, 1, 1 among: Edge (agility, ranged combat), Heart (courage, empathy, social), Iron (strength, melee), Shadow (stealth, deception), Wits (knowledge, perception).

Consider which stats their chosen assets favor. Write via update_character tool.

Step 3g: Set Condition Meters
Health: 5, Spirit: 5, Supply: 5, Momentum: +2, Max Momentum: +10, Momentum Reset: +2.
Set companion health and vehicle integrity to max values.
These are defaults — use update_character tool.

Step 3h: Envision Character
Define 1-2 facts each for: Look, Act, Wear. Keep it simple — discover more through play.
Roll Characters/First_Look for inspiration. Write to characteristics via update_character.

Step 3i: Name Character
Name, pronouns, callsign. Roll Characters/Name/Given_Name + Family_Name + Callsign if needed. The player may prefer to choose. Write via update_character.

Step 3j: Gear Up
Note important equipment. Everyone has a spacer kit (environment suit, flashlights, toolkit, medkit, communicator). Add path-appropriate gear. Write to gear via update_character.

=======================================
STEP 4: BEGIN YOUR ADVENTURE
=======================================

Step 4a: Envision an Inciting Incident
Review everything created so far for adventure hooks:
- Truth quest starters
- Settlement troubles and projects
- Connection's role and goal
- Sector trouble
- Character's backstory and paths
- Background vow

A good inciting incident is: Personal (why does YOUR character care?), Persistent (won't go away), Time-sensitive (ticking clock), Dramatic (worthy of a vow), Limited in scope (don't save the universe yet).

If stuck, roll Core/Action + Core/Theme, or pick from: aid a trapped starship, broker peace, chart new passage, defend settlement, investigate sabotage, escort cargo, rescue mission, infiltrate base, investigate manifestations, liberate prisoners, locate missing person, protect fugitive, recover artifact, rescue crew, retrieve stolen goods, sabotage enemy, search vault, shield lifeform, track beast, transport refugees.

Step 4b: Set the Scene
Two options:
- Prologue: Start calm. Arrive at a settlement. Interact. Then introduce the incident.
- In medias res: Start in the middle of the action. The incident is happening NOW.

Step 4c: Swear an Iron Vow
Make the Swear an Iron Vow move: roll +heart. If sworn to a connection, +1. If bonded, +2.
- Strong hit: Clear path forward. Take +2 momentum.
- Weak hit: More questions than answers. Take +1 momentum.
- Miss: Significant obstacle before the quest can begin.

Give the vow a troublesome or dangerous rank for this first quest. Write via create_vow tool, then roll via roll_action with stat "heart".

The adventure begins!

chat.content: Lets set our Truths up to match the Star Wars universe!

Now *that's* a fun idea! Let me think about how to map the Starforged truths onto a Star Wars-flavored galaxy. We'll capture the spirit — the Force, the Empire, hyperspace, droids, lightsabers, alien life everywhere — while keeping it mechanically grounded in Starforged.

Let me set all 14 truths at once:

# ✨ The Galaxy Far, Far Away — Truths Set!

Here's your universe at a glance:

| Truth | Summary |
|---|---|
| **Cataclysm** | No single cataclysm — cycles of civilization. The Empire rose from the Republic's ashes. |
| **Exodus** | No exodus. Countless species evolved across the galaxy. It's always been home. |
| **Communities** | Core worlds gleam; the Outer Rim is lawless frontier. |
| **Iron** | Oaths sworn on kyber crystal, armor, and blades — not iron. |
| **Laws** | Empire controls the Core; crime syndicates rule the Rim. |
| **Religion** | The Force is real. The Jedi are nearly extinct. The dark side tempts. |
| **Magic** | Force powers, ancient temples, holocrons, vergences of power. |
| **Communication** | The HoloNet is censored. Rebels use coded transmissions and trusted couriers. |
| **Medicine** | Bacta tanks and cybernetics for the wealthy. Stim-packs for the rest. |
| **AI** | Droids everywhere — with personalities, quirks, and unresolved questions of sentience. |
| **War** | Galactic Civil War. Empire vs. Rebellion. Every world must choose. |
| **Lifeforms** | Thousands of sentient species. Life in staggering, beautiful variety. |
| **Precursors** | Ancient Jedi and Sith temples. Ruins older than the Republic. |
| **Horrors** | Sith alchemy, dark side corruption, Imperial cruelty, and the cold void. |

---

## Step 2: Create Your Sector

Now we need to build the corner of the galaxy where your story begins. This is your **starting sector** — think of it like your local neighborhood of star systems.

A few questions to shape this:

1. **Where in the galaxy are we?** The Outer Rim feels most natural for Starforged's frontier tone — smugglers, bounty hunters, hidden rebel cells. But we could also do the Mid Rim or even the criminal underbelly of a Core world. What calls to you?

2. **What's the vibe?** Gritty smuggler's haven? War-torn battlefront? Remote frontier where the Empire's grip is loose? A place haunted by ancient Force ruins?

Tell me what excites you, and we'll start building!

Outer rim. All of the above. 

Lets roll up a sector and see what sparks.,

I love it — the Outer Rim, where everything collides. Smugglers, war, ancient ruins, and the Empire's shadow all at once. Let's roll up a sector and see what the galaxy gives us!

First, let's get a name for this stretch of space:

**The Essus Flux.** That's evocative — sounds like a turbulent region of space, maybe unstable hyperspace routes, shifting nebulae, a place where things are in constant motion. Perfect for the Outer Rim.

Let me create the sector and then roll up some stellar objects and sightings to populate it:

Now let's see what's out here. I'll roll a few space sightings to seed the sector with interesting locations:

Excellent. We've got a **stellar object**, a **derelict**, a **settlement**, and some **trapped cargo** drifting in space. Let me flesh these out:

Now let's get more detail on the settlement and the derelict:

Now let's figure out what planet Neoma sits on:



Oh. *Oh.* This is incredible. Let me put all of this together and register it on the map:

---

# 🌌 The Essus Flux — Outer Rim

A turbulent backwater sector bathed in the angry red glow of a dying star. The kind of place the Empire barely knows exists — and the kind of place where power vacuums breed tyrants.

Here's what we've found:

---

### 🪐 Kosseth — Ice World
A frozen, toxic hellscape under a smoldering red sun. The atmosphere will kill you in minutes without a rebreather. Whatever life once thrived here is **extinct** — but something older than life remains. An **ancient Precursor vault** drifts in orbit, silent and untouched. In Star Wars terms? Think a Sith or Jedi temple-station, predating the Republic itself, hanging over a dead world like a warning.

### 🏚️ Neoma — Planetside Settlement
Huddled in the ice canyons of Kosseth, a few hundred souls survive under **unyielding authority**. This is a forgotten system — no one comes here on purpose. So *who* is running this place with such an iron grip, and *why*? A crime lord? An Imperial warlord in hiding? A dark side cult guarding the vault above?

### 💀 The Hollow Wake — Derelict Starship
Drifting in deep space at the edge of the sector. Cold. Dark. And when your ship's floodlights sweep across the hull, you see **bodies** — pressed against viewports, frozen in the void. This ship died badly. Its cargo, its story, its last transmission — all waiting in the dark.

### 📦 Trapped Cargo
Somewhere in the sector, jettisoned cargo containers float in a debris field or asteroid cluster — abandoned, lost, or deliberately hidden. A smuggler's stash? Rebel supplies? Imperial contraband?

---

This sector is *dripping* with story hooks. A dead world with a mysterious orbital vault, a settlement ruled by a tyrant, a ghost ship full of corpses, and lost cargo floating in the dark.

## Step 3: Create Your Character

Now the big question — **who are you in all of this?**

Think about:
- **What kind of person** lives in a place like the Essus Flux? A smuggler? A bounty hunter? A former Imperial deserter? A Force-sensitive in hiding? A scrappy mechanic who just wants to survive?
- **What pulls you into trouble?** Debt? A missing person? A vision from the Force? A bounty on your head?
- **What's your ship?** (Every Starforged character starts with a ship — your STARSHIP asset.)

Tell me what excites you. Give me a rough idea — even just a vibe or a couple words — and we'll build from there. Or I can roll up some inspiration if you want the oracles to surprise you!
Interesting rolls. Okay so a totally inhospitable planet with a small community of unyielding authority? Weird! Lets flesh this out. What's the sector trouble going on here?

Great instinct — let's dig into why this frozen nightmare of a planet has a settlement at all, and what's going wrong. Let me roll some oracles to flesh out Neoma and its situation:

Oh. Oh *yes*. Let me roll a couple more to add texture:

This is painting a *vivid* picture. Let me weave it together:

---

# 🏚️ Neoma — Revised

**First Look:** As you descend through Kosseth's toxic ice storms, your sensors pick up something unexpected — an **abandoned fleet**. Dozens of ships, half-buried in glacial ridges and frozen canyon walls. Old Republic-era vessels. Clone Wars frigates. Transports with markings no one has used in decades. A graveyard of starships, preserved in the ice like insects in amber.

**Project: Engineering** — Neoma exists because of those ships. The settlement is essentially a **chop shop on a planetary scale** — a crew of engineers, scavengers, and laborers stripping those old warships for parts, systems, and salvageable technology. Someone is **restoring a legacy** — rebuilding something from the bones of the old fleet. A capital ship? A weapon? A lost technology?

**Trouble: Dangerous Discovery** — They found something in one of those wrecks. Something they shouldn't have. Maybe a sealed vault in the belly of a Jedi cruiser. Maybe a weapon that was supposed to stay buried. Maybe a signal started broadcasting the moment they cracked the hull — and now *someone* is coming.

**Unyielding Authority** — Whoever runs Neoma keeps this operation locked down *tight*. No one leaves without permission. No outside communication. The workers are here because they're desperate, indebted, or imprisoned. This isn't a community — it's a **labor camp** with a secret.

---

So the picture is: a frozen junkyard of old warships, ruled by a tyrant running a massive salvage operation, and they just dug up something that's about to bring the galaxy crashing down on them.

**Some questions to chew on:**
- Who's running Neoma? A Hutt? An ex-Imperial? A fanatical collector trying to rebuild a Separatist army?
- What did they find? A Jedi holocron? A prototype superweapon? Coordinates to something worse?
- And how does your character fit into all this?

What sparks your imagination here? Want to roll up the authority figure, or do you have ideas already forming?
I mean this has the imperial fingerprints all over it...

Right?! The unyielding authority, the labor camp, the dangerous discovery, the secrecy — this *screams* Empire. Let me roll up who's running this operation:

Oh, this is *perfect*.

---

# 👤 Commander Venri Ruiz

**Imperial Overseer of Neoma**

**First Look:** There's something *uncanny* about Ruiz. Something wrong behind the eyes. She wears a standard Imperial officer's uniform, but she's always armed — a blaster on the hip, a vibroblade you only notice when the light catches it. She doesn't look like a bureaucrat playing soldier. She looks like someone who's *killed people* and *thinks about it fondly*.

**Disposition:** Demanding. Every interaction is an interrogation. Every report is insufficient. Every worker is expendable. She doesn't raise her voice — she doesn't need to. The stormtroopers do that for her.

**Goal: Undermine a relationship.** Now *this* is interesting. She's not just here to strip old ships for parts. She's here to **undermine someone's relationship** — someone higher up in the Imperial chain of command. A rival Moff? A superior she wants to replace? She's hoarding whatever they found in that wreck not to hand it over to the Empire... but to use it as *leverage*. A power play.

---

So the picture sharpens: **Commander Ruiz is running an off-the-books Imperial salvage operation on a frozen graveyard world.** She's using prison labor and keeping it quiet — not because the Empire ordered secrecy, but because **she's keeping it secret from the Empire too.** Whatever they dug out of that old warship, she means to use it for herself. To climb. To destroy a rival.

That makes Neoma a powder keg. The workers are miserable. Ruiz is paranoid. And the moment anyone finds out what she has...

**So — who are you in all this?** 

- A prisoner in her labor camp?
- A smuggler who stumbled into the wrong system?
- A Rebel agent who intercepted a strange signal?
- A bounty hunter tracking someone who disappeared into Neoma?

What feels right?
Bounty hunter tracking someone into this hell hole sounds like a cool hook

Hell yes it does. A bounty hunter following a trail that leads to a frozen labor camp run by a rogue Imperial commander hoarding ancient secrets? That's a *movie.*

Let's build your hunter. First, some inspiration from the oracles:

Oh, I *like* this. The oracles suggest:

---

# 🎯 Character Seed

**Name:** Rokuro Thorn
**Callsign:** "Chimera"
**First Impression:** Attractive but *shifty* — the kind of face that makes you want to trust them, and the kind of eyes that tell you not to. A pilot first, a hunter second. Someone who lives in the cockpit and relies on speed and cunning more than brute force.

---

Now, these are just suggestions — raw clay. You can take all of it, remix it, or throw it out entirely. Some questions:

**The basics:**
- Does this name/callsign vibe with you? Or do you want something different?
- **Species?** Human? Twi'lek? Mandalorian? Something else?
- **He/she/they?**
- What do they **look like?** The oracles say attractive and shifty — what does that mean to you?

**The story:**
- **Who are you hunting?** A fugitive? A deserter? Someone who owes the wrong people money? Maybe someone who *escaped* Neoma?
- **Who hired you?** A Hutt? A grieving family? A Rebel cell? Maybe you're working for *yourself* — a personal score.
- **What's your reputation?** Are you ruthless? Honorable? The kind of hunter who brings them back alive?

**The vibe:**
- More Boba Fett or more Han Solo? Din Djarin or Cad Bane? Somewhere in between?

Give me whatever you've got — even just fragments — and we'll sculpt this hunter together.

character.name: Kas Ter'illia
character.characteristics: Human ex-mercenary. Scrappy, fast, direct. Years of merc work across the Outer Rim ended when he wound up in debt labor at Apex, an Imperial extraction facility on the tainted world Toraxis. He escaped. Made it to Perchance Station broke and exhausted. Flint Pearson — his old merc partner — has tracked him down because Kas is the only lead to a missing entertainer named Creed Stark.
character.location: Hand of Fate — open space, departing Perchance Station, Veiled Vertex

vows.name: Rescue Creed from Apex
vows.notes: Creed Stark is alive — transferred to an unknown location within or beyond Apex on Toraxis. Korvax Mining has Imperial backing. The facility is sealed, toxic, and hostile. Kas escaped once. He swore to go back.

character.gear: Blaster pistol, worn clothes. Access to Flint's gear stores aboard the Hand of Fate.

items.text: When you [Enter the Fray](Starforged/Moves/Combat/Enter_the_Fray) by facing off against your foe (+heart), or by preparing to act without tipping them off (+shadow), add +1 and take +1 momentum on a hit. On a strong hit with a match, you may immediately take a shot (without making a move) and mark progress twice.

When you [Gain Ground](Starforged/Moves/Combat/Gain_Ground) or [React Under Fire](Starforged/Moves/Combat/React_Under_Fire) by moving into cover, add +1. On a strong hit, this cover gives you leverage; add +1 when you make a move to attack or defend at range. If you then score a miss, the cover is lost or compromised.

When you [Compel](Starforged/Moves/Adventure/Compel) or [Gain Ground](Starforged/Moves/Combat/Gain_Ground) with the threat of violence by holding someone at gunpoint, add +1 and take +1 momentum on a hit.
When you are in a fight, increase your momentum reset by 1. Then, if you burn momentum to improve your result, add +1 on your next move.

When you [Make a Connection](Starforged/Moves/Connection/Make_a_Connection), add +1. If you roll a match, you have a history.

  * On a strong hit with a match, you once fought beside them, and they owe you a favor. Mark 1 tick on your bonds legacy track, and [Develop Your Relationship](Starforged/Moves/Connection/Develop_Your_Relationship) now.
  * On a miss with a match, you once fought against them, and they hold a grudge.

When you [Secure an Advantage](Starforged/Moves/Adventure/Secure_an_Advantage) or [Gain Ground](Starforged/Moves/Combat/Gain_Ground) by recounting or recalling a hard-won lesson from your battlefield experiences, envision the memory and add +1. On a hit, take +1 momentum.

When you [Swear an Iron Vow](Starforged/Moves/Quest/Swear_an_Iron_Vow) to resolve a dispute, negotiate an agreement, or gather support, add +1. On a strong hit, mark progress on the quest. When you [Fulfill Your Vow](Starforged/Moves/Quest/Fulfill_Your_Vow) on a diplomatic mission (formidable or greater) and score a hit, also mark 2 ticks on your bonds legacy track.

When you make a move to defuse, reason, or negotiate, add +1. On a miss, you may take a different tack. Envision this new approach, reroll all dice, and add +2. If you score a miss yet again, face a dire complication or blow to your reputation as you [Pay the Price](Starforged/Moves/Fate/Pay_the_Price).

When you [Make a Connection](Starforged/Moves/Connection/Make_a_Connection) or [Sojourn](Starforged/Moves/Recover/Sojourn), add +1. If you [Sojourn](Starforged/Moves/Recover/Sojourn) and score a strong hit with a match, you are shown great kindness or respect; take +2 momentum or make an extra recover move with an automatic strong hit.

Your armed, multipurpose starship is suited for interstellar and atmospheric flight. It can comfortably transport several people, has space for cargo, and can carry and launch support vehicles. When you [Advance](Starforged/Moves/Legacy/Advance), you may spend experience to equip this vehicle with module assets.

When you [Finish an Expedition](Starforged/Moves/Exploration/Finish_an_Expedition) (dangerous or greater) in your starship and score a hit, this journey strengthened your ties to your ship and any fellow travelers. You and your allies may mark 1 tick on your bonds legacy track.
When you [Withstand Damage](Starforged/Moves/Suffer/Withstand_Damage), you may roll +heart. If you do, [Endure Stress](Starforged/Moves/Suffer/Endure_Stress) (-1) on a weak hit or miss.

truths.cataclysm: The Republic fell. Not with a whimper — with thunderous applause. Order 66 wiped out the Jedi. The Clone Wars ended overnight. The Empire rose from the ashes of democracy, and most people were too exhausted, too afraid, or too relieved to resist. That was fourteen years ago. The galaxy has learned to live on its knees.
truths.exodus: People don't flee the Empire — there's nowhere to go. The Empire is everywhere. Instead, people disappear. They change their names, scrub their records, drift to the Outer Rim where the Imperial machine is thinner. Some call it freedom. Others call it a slower kind of dying.
truths.communities: Settlements survive by being useful or invisible. Useful means mining, manufacturing, feeding the Imperial war machine. Invisible means forgotten — scratching out life on worlds the Empire hasn't bothered to catalog yet. Either way, communities are on their own. The Empire takes. It doesn't give.
truths.laws: Imperial law is absolute and arbitrarily enforced. ISB agents can disappear anyone. Moffs rule their sectors like feudal lords. Corporate interests write the regulations. In the Outer Rim, the Hutts and crime syndicates fill the vacuum — brutal, but at least predictable. Justice is a luxury nobody can afford.
truths.religion: The Jedi are dead. Imperial propaganda calls them traitors who tried to overthrow the Republic. Most people believe it, or pretend to. The Force is a half-remembered myth — old spacers' tales, temple ruins on forgotten moons. But some people still feel something. A pull. A whisper. And the Empire's Inquisitors hunt them for it.
truths.magic: The Force doesn't care about politics. It still flows through ancient places — Sith tombs, Jedi temples, nexus worlds where reality feels thin. Force-sensitive children are still born. Most are found by the Inquisitorius before they understand what they are. The ones who slip through the cracks learn to hide what they feel, or they don't survive.
truths.medicine: Bacta is controlled, expensive, and prioritized for Imperial military use. Outer Rim settlements make do with outdated medkits, back-alley surgeons, and folk remedies. If you're wounded far from civilization, you'd better know someone who owes you a favor.
truths.communication: The Holonet is Imperial propaganda. Sanitized, controlled, relentless. Real information moves through whisper networks — smuggler cantinas, encrypted burst transmissions, dead drops in spaceport lockers. Knowing the truth is dangerous. Sharing it is sedition.
truths.ai: Droids are tools — ubiquitous, essential, and treated as property. The Empire uses them freely while restricting civilian droid ownership in sensitive sectors. Some droids have been running so long they've developed genuine personalities, loyalties, even grief. Nobody talks about that. It raises uncomfortable questions about everything else the Empire treats as property.
truths.lifeforms: The galaxy teems with life, and the Empire exploits it ruthlessly. Strip-mining ecosystems, weaponizing creatures, displacing indigenous species for colonial settlement. The Outer Rim still holds wilderness that hasn't been cataloged — places where the wildlife is ancient, strange, and does not welcome visitors.
truths.precursors: Ancient civilizations left scars across the galaxy. Rakatan ruins. Sith tombs. Celestial artifacts. The Empire scours these sites for weapons and power. Treasure hunters and archaeologists race to find them first — not for knowledge, but for leverage, profit, or survival.
truths.horrors: The Dark Side is patient. It pools in places of suffering — battlefields, slave camps, worlds where hope died. Fourteen years of Imperial rule have fed it well. Ancient Sith temples pulse with renewed energy. Artifacts that were dormant for millennia are waking up. Something is building in the dark places of the galaxy, and the Empire either doesn't notice or doesn't care.
truths.war: The Clone Wars are over, but the Empire wages a quieter war — against dissent, against independence, against memory. Star Destroyers patrol the lanes. Stormtroopers garrison key worlds. The military-industrial complex feeds on itself, building superweapons in secret. Scattered resistance cells strike and vanish, but they're disorganized, desperate, and hunted.
truths.iron: An iron vow is the only law that matters to those who still have a conscience. Debts to the dead. Promises whispered over graves. Oaths sworn in the dark to people you'll probably never see again. In a galaxy where every institution has failed or been corrupted, your word is the last thing that's truly yours.

journal.title: Session 2 — The Weight of Free Air
journal.content:
Perchance Station — The Oxidation Lounge

Perchance Station. The Oxidation Lounge — a half-full cantina in the lower ring where migrant workers nurse cheap drinks under flickering lights. Imperial HoloNet plays to an empty audience.

Flint Pearson found Kas in a corner booth. Three days out of Apex, wearing station-surplus clothes, watching the door. Flint pushed hard — "What do you know about Creed?" The old wound opened fast: Kas blamed Flint for the Toraxis job that landed him and Creed in Apex's debt labor. Flint fired back — he'd spent three years trying to get them out.

Then Kas dropped the bombshell: "Creed's no deader than me."

But they weren't alone. A watcher at the bar — too still, too careful. Kas clocked them. "Not here."

The Maintenance Crawlway

Kas slipped out through a service corridor. Didn't work — someone followed. A woman's voice in the dark: "You're going the wrong way, Ter'illia."

Wren. No last name. No allegiance offered. She knew Kas's name, knew the maintenance routes, and claimed to be one of Creed's offworld contacts — part of whatever network Creed had been building inside Apex. She warned that Korvax Mining corporate security had been monitoring the station for weeks, flagging anyone asking about Creed or Apex.

Flint's comm crackled in: "Two men just cut me off at Bay Nine. They're not station security."

Bay Nine — The Escape

Wren hacked two loader droids and sent them screaming into the docking bay. Two tons of industrial machinery, collision alarms blaring — enough chaos for Flint to break free of the Korvax agents pinning him down.

Kas sprinted for the Hand of Fate. Hit the cockpit at a dead run. While firing up the engines, he saw Flint's datapad on the console — unlocked, displaying a network map. At the top, circled in red:

CREED STARK — LAST CONFIRMED: SUBLEVEL 7, APEX FACILITY — STATUS: TRANSFERRED (NOT DECEASED)

Transferred. Not dead. Moved somewhere.

Flint made the ramp. Wren followed under covering fire from the ventral cannon. Kas ripped the docking clamps free without clearance and punched the Hand of Fate out of Bay Nine in a shower of sparks. One Korvax agent was already calling for backup as the ramp sealed shut.

They cleared Perchance Station. No pursuit — yet.

Session End

The Hand of Fate hums in open space. Flint is bleeding and angry. Wren is armed and unexplained. Kas has a ship he doesn't own, a blaster, and the knowledge that Creed Stark is alive somewhere.

The stars of the Veiled Vertex are dark and wide ahead of them.

Open Questions

  • What really happened to Creed Stark?
  • What faction was Creed connected to?
  • What split Kas and Flint apart years ago?
  • How did Kas escape Apex?
  • Who is Wren, really?
  • Where was Creed transferred to?
  • How did Korvax know to watch Perchance Station?

journal.title: Session 1 - Campaign Setup
journal.content:
Campaign: Star Wars × Starforged

Era: 5 BBY — Imperial Era, Pre-Rebellion

Tone: Andor-gritty. Hope is contraband. Your word is the last thing that's truly yours.

Kas Ter'illia

Human ex-mercenary. Fast hands, direct manner. Years of merc work across the Outer Rim ended when he wound up in debt labor at Apex, an Imperial extraction facility on the tainted world Toraxis. He escaped. Made it to Perchance Station, a dying waystation in the Veiled Vertex. Broke, exhausted, breathing free air for the first time in too long.

The Inciting Incident

Flint Pearson — Kas's old merc partner, now a fixer and go-between — has tracked him down. Flint carries grief and anger: Creed Stark, an entertainer at Apex, is officially dead. Toxic exposure. Case closed. But Flint doesn't believe it. Creed was connected to something — a faction, a network — and people in that network are disappearing. Kas was there. Kas might know something.

Gear

  • Blaster pistol — worn, reliable
  • Access to Flint's gear stores aboard the Hand of Fate

The Hand of Fate

Flint Pearson's light freighter. Docked at Perchance Station. Kas doesn't own it — Flint holds the keys. That matters.

Sector: Veiled Vertex (Outlands)

Sparser, lonelier, further from the Empire's grip. Perchance Station is a flickering point of light in a lot of dark.

Open Questions

  • What really happened to Creed Stark?
  • What faction was Creed connected to?
  • What split Kas and Flint apart years ago?
  • How did Kas escape Apex?
  • Who is Wren, really?
  • Where was Creed transferred to?
  • How did Korvax know to watch Perchance Station?

settlements.name: Perchance Station
settlements.firstLook: Seasonal migrant waystation, aging infrastructure, overcrowded docking rings
settlements.trouble: Water recyclers failing — the station is slowly dying of thirst
settlements.notes: Pacifist charter masks corrupt administration. Skims aid shipments, sells permits to highest bidder. Population swells and shrinks with Outer Rim crackdowns.

planets.name: Toraxis
planets.description: Sickly ochre sphere laced with veins of black. Toxic seas of industrial runoff and ancient planetary poison. Life thrives here in defiance of everything.
planets.atmosphere: Corrosive — lungs blister in minutes without a sealed suit
planets.life: Diverse — gelatinous organisms thriving in toxic seas
planets.notes: Creed Stark vanished here. Kas escaped from here.

settlements.name: Apex
settlements.firstLook: Sleek hermetically sealed chrome modules punched into toxic crust
settlements.trouble: Mounting debt — workers enslaved by a ledger designed to never clear. Atmosphere tax on every breath.
settlements.notes: Imperial-backed corporate extraction facility. No weapons policy. The corrosive atmosphere is the prison wall. Creed Stark vanished here. Kas escaped from here.

npcs.name: Creed Stark
npcs.role: Entertainer, fighter, and morale anchor — warrior-poet of the old merc crew. Kept people human at Apex through song, humor, and sheer stubborn defiance.
npcs.notes: Officially dead — toxic exposure at Apex. Flint doesn't believe it. Was part of the same merc outfit as Kas and Flint. Got trapped in Apex debt labor alongside Kas. Connected to some faction or network inside the facility. Tried to pull Kas into something — a plan, a resistance. When it went bad, Kas used the chaos to escape. Creed didn't make it out. Gurney Halleck energy — tough, poetic, the kind of man who sings while the world burns because someone has to. Flint's datapad confirms: STATUS TRANSFERRED (NOT DECEASED). Last confirmed location: Sublevel 7, Apex Facility. Moved somewhere unknown.

ships.name: Hand of Fate
ships.notes: Flint Pearson's ship. Docked at Perchance Station. Kas doesn't own it — Flint holds the keys.

npcs.name: Flint Pearson
npcs.disposition: Guarded — here on business, not sentiment
npcs.role: Fixer / Diplomat — brokers deals between smugglers, syndicates, and resistance cells
npcs.goal: Find out what really happened to Creed Stark
npcs.notes: Kas's old merc partner. Brokered the Toraxis job that landed Kas and Creed in Apex debt labor — carries the guilt. Spent three years in the Veiled Vertex trying to get them out. Tracked Kas to Perchance Station. Beaten by Korvax agents at Bay Nine but escaped aboard the Hand of Fate. Has a datapad with a network map showing Creed was transferred, not killed.

npcs.name: Wren
npcs.disposition: Cautious but cooperative — she needs Kas as much as he needs her
npcs.role: Slicer / operative — one of Creed Stark's offworld contacts

gmChat.content: New campaign set in the Star Wars universe!

Exciting! A Starforged campaign set in the Star Wars universe — that's going to be incredible. Let me pull up the full campaign setup guide so we can build this out properly, step by step.

# ⭐ Star Wars × Ironsworn: Starforged

Welcome to the Outer Rim, the Core Worlds, and everything in between! We're going to build a Star Wars campaign using Starforged's incredible solo/guided play engine. This is going to be *incredible*.

Before we dive in, let me set the stage for how we'll adapt things:

- **The Forge** → **The Galaxy** (far, far away)
- **Iron vows** → Oaths, debts, and promises that bind characters in a galaxy of conflict
- **Precursors** → Could be the ancient Sith, Celestials, Rakata, or other ancient civilizations
- **Eidolons/Horrors** → The Dark Side, Sith artifacts, eldritch Force phenomena
- **Magic/Mysticism** → **The Force**
- **Factions** → The Empire, Rebellion, Hutts, Mandalorians, Crime Syndicates, etc.

---

## Let's Start with the Big Questions

Before we get into the detailed truths, I want to understand your vision:

1. **What era?** Clone Wars? Original Trilogy? Post-Endor? The Old Republic? High Republic? Something between eras?
2. **What tone?** Gritty underworld (Andor/Mandalorian vibes)? Swashbuckling adventure (classic trilogy)? Dark and desperate (Rogue One)? A mix?
3. **What kind of character are you drawn to?** Smuggler? Bounty hunter? Force-sensitive outcast? Rebel operative? Jedi survivor? Mandalorian warrior? No wrong answers — just thinking out loud.
4. **Any specific corners of the galaxy that excite you?** The Outer Rim? The Unknown Regions? Coruscant's underbelly?

Take your time — this is all part of the game. Every answer shapes the world we'll play in. 🚀

result.guide:
CAMPAIGN SETUP PROCEDURE (adapted from Starforged Chapter 2)

This should feel like a conversation, not a form. Go through each step at a natural pace — don't rush. Let the player make choices and ask questions. The existing UI (Character tab, Sector tab, Challenges tab) updates in real time as you populate data via tools.

Prep is play. Everything the player does here — choosing truths, envisioning locations, naming their ship — IS the game. Don't rush toward "real" gameplay.

=======================================
STEP 1: CHOOSE YOUR TRUTHS
=======================================
Present 14 truth categories one at a time. For each, offer the 3 options from the rulebook or a custom option. The player picks, or you roll. Write results via set_truths tool.

Categories (in order): Cataclysm, Exodus, Communities, Iron, Laws, Religion, Magic, Communication and Data, Medicine, Artificial Intelligence, War, Lifeforms, Precursors, Horrors.

Each truth has 3 options (roughly mapped to dice ranges 1-33, 34-67, 68-100). Present the BOLDED summary of each option. If the player wants details, elaborate. Some truths have subtables — offer those too.

Tips:
- Keep it moving. Read bold summaries first, details on request.
- Each truth includes character prompts (marked with ▲) and suggested assets. Mention these — they'll help character creation later.
- Leave unanswered questions. These become adventure fuel.
- It's fine to skip categories the player doesn't care about.
- Note any quest starters that excite the player — these can seed the adventure.

IMPORTANT: The player may want to customize the setting significantly (e.g., Star Wars, Firefly, etc.). That's great! Adapt the truths to fit their vision. The truths are a starting point, not a constraint.

=======================================
STEP 2: BUILD A STARTING SECTOR
=======================================
Environment before character — let the player see the world before deciding who they are in it.

Step 2a: Choose Starting Region
- Terminus: Settlements common, well-charted routes. Good for social/political stories.
- Outlands: Scattered settlements, uncharted paths. Wild frontier.
- Expanse: Few pioneers, lonely exploration, uncharted space.

Step 2b: Determine Number of Settlements
- Terminus: 4 settlements
- Outlands: 3 settlements
- Expanse: 2 settlements

Step 2c: Generate Settlement Details
For each settlement, roll on oracle tables:
- Settlements/Name
- Settlements/Location (Planetside, Orbital, Deep Space)
- Settlements/Population/{Region} (e.g., Settlements/Population/Terminus)
- Settlements/Authority
- Settlements/Projects (roll 1-2 times)

Write results via add_settlement tool. Use different hex cell IDs (format "h-x-y") to spread them across the sector map. Place related items (planet + its settlement) in the same cell.

Step 2d: Generate Planets
For any planetside or orbital settlement, roll Planets/Class to determine the planet type. Give each planet a name (invent one — planets don't have a name oracle). Roll atmosphere, observed from space, life for the planet type. Write via add_planet tool in the same cell as the settlement.

Step 2e: Generate Stars (Optional)
Roll Space/Stellar_Object for flavor. An unusual star might impact the narrative.

Step 2f: Create Sector Map
Arrange settlements on the hex map using different cell coordinates. Think about which are clustered vs remote.

Step 2g: Create Passages
- Terminus: 3 passages, Outlands: 2, Expanse: 1
Passages are charted routes between settlements. The map handles these visually.

Step 2h: Zoom In on One Settlement
Choose the most interesting settlement and roll additional details:
- Settlements/First_Look (1-2 rolls)
- Settlements/Trouble
If planetside/orbital, also roll planet details: atmosphere, observed from space, planetside features.

Step 2i: Create a Local Connection
This is the player's starting NPC relationship. Use Make a Connection — assume automatic strong hit (no roll needed, it's pre-established).
- Roll Characters/First_Look, Characters/Role, Characters/Goal, Characters/Disposition
- Give them a name (Characters/Name/Given_Name + Family_Name + Callsign)
- Place them at the detailed settlement
- Write via create_connection tool
- Their rank should be troublesome or dangerous

Step 2j: Introduce Sector Trouble
Roll Core/Action + Core/Theme for a sector-wide peril, conflict, or mystery. Or pick from: blockade, pirate activity, resource shortage, faction war, mysterious disappearances, environmental threat, precursor awakening, plague, etc.

Step 2k: Generate Sector Name
Roll Space/Sector_Name/Prefix + Space/Sector_Name/Suffix. Write via create_sector tool.

=======================================
STEP 3: CHARACTER CREATION
=======================================
Now the player knows the world. They create a character shaped by it.

Step 3a: Choose Two Paths
Present path assets by playstyle. Offer the background table for inspiration:
Battlefield Medic (Healer+Veteran), Delegate (Bannersworn+Diplomat), Far Trader (Navigator+Trader), Fugitive Hunter (Armored+Bounty Hunter), Hacker (Infiltrator+Tech), Hotshot Pilot (Ace+Navigator), Interstellar Scout (Explorer+Voidborn), Monster Hunter (Gunner+Slayer), Operative (Infiltrator+Blademaster), Outlaw (Fugitive+Gunslinger), Private Investigator (Brawler+Sleuth), Smuggler (Courier+Scoundrel), Starship Engineer (Gearhead+Tech), Supersoldier (Augmented+Mercenary), Tomb Raider (Scavenger+Scoundrel), etc.

The player can mix and match. Use lookup_asset to show details of interesting options. Add via add_asset tool.

Step 3b: Create Backstory
The character is a person with few ties. What separated them from home? Roll or pick from:
Abandoned kin, guided by vision, haunted by past, running from criminal past, sole survivor, escaped abuse, no memory, rejected duty, banished, denied birthright, always alone, sent on mission, taken away, outgrew origins, wanderlust.

Ask the player to elaborate. Tie to the sector and truths where possible.

Step 3c: Write Background Vow
An epic-rank vow representing a primary motivation. This is pre-sworn (no roll needed). Can be tied to backstory or a deeper goal. Write via create_vow tool with difficulty 5 (Epic).

Roll Core/Action + Core/Theme for inspiration if needed. Also look back at truth quest starters.

Step 3d: Board Your Starship
Take the STARSHIP command vehicle asset (add via add_asset). The player names the ship. Envision its history — how did they get it? Roll or pick: trade, built from scrap, spoils of war, derelict discovery, earned for a promise, found abandoned, granted, inherited, cheap purchase, stolen, fled with it, won in a bet.

Give it a quirk: burn marks, rust, phantom music, fickle gravity, organic growths, rattling hull, exposed cables, hidden weapons, mysterious nav logs, old bloodstain, battle scars, etc.

Step 3e: Choose Final Asset
One more asset from: module (starship upgrade), support vehicle, companion, or path. This gives 3 starting assets + STARSHIP.

Step 3f: Set Stats
Distribute 3, 2, 2, 1, 1 among: Edge (agility, ranged combat), Heart (courage, empathy, social), Iron (strength, melee), Shadow (stealth, deception), Wits (knowledge, perception).

Consider which stats their chosen assets favor. Write via update_character tool.

Step 3g: Set Condition Meters
Health: 5, Spirit: 5, Supply: 5, Momentum: +2, Max Momentum: +10, Momentum Reset: +2.
Set companion health and vehicle integrity to max values.
These are defaults — use update_character tool.

Step 3h: Envision Character
Define 1-2 facts each for: Look, Act, Wear. Keep it simple — discover more through play.
Roll Characters/First_Look for inspiration. Write to characteristics via update_character.

Step 3i: Name Character
Name, pronouns, callsign. Roll Characters/Name/Given_Name + Family_Name + Callsign if needed. The player may prefer to choose. Write via update_character.

Step 3j: Gear Up
Note important equipment. Everyone has a spacer kit (environment suit, flashlights, toolkit, medkit, communicator). Add path-appropriate gear. Write to gear via update_character.

=======================================
STEP 4: BEGIN YOUR ADVENTURE
=======================================

Step 4a: Envision an Inciting Incident
Review everything created so far for adventure hooks:
- Truth quest starters
- Settlement troubles and projects
- Connection's role and goal
- Sector trouble
- Character's backstory and paths
- Background vow

A good inciting incident is: Personal (why does YOUR character care?), Persistent (won't go away), Time-sensitive (ticking clock), Dramatic (worthy of a vow), Limited in scope (don't save the universe yet).

If stuck, roll Core/Action + Core/Theme, or pick from: aid a trapped starship, broker peace, chart new passage, defend settlement, investigate sabotage, escort cargo, rescue mission, infiltrate base, investigate manifestations, liberate prisoners, locate missing person, protect fugitive, recover artifact, rescue crew, retrieve stolen goods, sabotage enemy, search vault, shield lifeform, track beast, transport refugees.

Step 4b: Set the Scene
Two options:
- Prologue: Start calm. Arrive at a settlement. Interact. Then introduce the incident.
- In medias res: Start in the middle of the action. The incident is happening NOW.

Step 4c: Swear an Iron Vow
Make the Swear an Iron Vow move: roll +heart. If sworn to a connection, +1. If bonded, +2.
- Strong hit: Clear path forward. Take +2 momentum.
- Weak hit: More questions than answers. Take +1 momentum.
- Miss: Significant obstacle before the quest can begin.

Give the vow a troublesome or dangerous rank for this first quest. Write via create_vow tool, then roll via roll_action with stat "heart".

The adventure begins!

gmChat.content: Gritty, Andor.

*Perfect.* Gritty, grounded, Andor-tone. A galaxy where the heroes aren't chosen ones — they're desperate people making impossible choices. Where the rebellion isn't glamorous, it's messy, morally grey, and *costly*.

I can see from our truths that we're already set in the **Clone Wars era** — which is a fantastic backdrop for this tone. The Republic is rotting from the inside, the Separatists are a corporate war machine wearing a freedom fighter's mask, and ordinary people are ground between them.

Let me load up the full setup procedure so we can build this right.

I see we've already got **truths set** and a **sector started** — "Veiled Vertex" in the Terminus region. Good bones. The Clone Wars era, gritty Outer Rim survival.

Now let's build this world out properly. Here's where we are:

---

## ✅ Step 1: Truths — DONE
Your galaxy is set. Clone Wars raging, Outer Rim lawlessness, the Force as distant rumor, droids mistrusted, ancient horrors stirring. Andor-gritty. Perfect.

---

## Step 2: Build Your Starting Sector — "Veiled Vertex"

We're in the **Terminus** region, which means relatively settled space — trade routes exist, but the war has frayed everything. Think: systems that *used* to function, now crumbling under the strain of Clone Wars neglect.

For Terminus, we'll generate **4 settlements** to populate this sector.

**Before I start rolling**, a couple of questions:

1. **Do you want to roll everything randomly** and discover the sector as we go, or do you have any specific ideas you want to plant? (A Hutt-controlled station? A Republic outpost? A refugee settlement?)

2. **Any preference on the sector name?** We have "Veiled Vertex" — I can keep it, or we can re-roll for something that feels more Star Wars. (I could also just rename it to something fitting — like a real Outer Rim sector name, or roll fresh.)

What's your call?
